title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying standards acceptable behavior. Enforcement responsibility Code Conduct Committee, take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported member Code Conduct Committee. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. Code Conduct Committee use Enforcement Manual determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct. community leaders Code Conduct Committee members obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to hubWebEvalsData
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). procedures contributing bigger changes, code particular, generally follow advised tidyverse dev team, including following tidyverse style guide code recording user facing changes NEWS.md.

title: Contributing to hubWebEvalsData
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("hubverse-org/hubWebEvalsData", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to hubWebEvalsData
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

what: Generate evaluation data for a hub — generate_eval_data
title: Generate evaluation data for a hub — generate_eval_data
text: Scores target configured predevals config oracle data writes wide-format score tables disk.

title: Generate evaluation data for a hub — generate_eval_data
Generate evaluation data for a hub — generate_eval_data
text: hub_path path hub. config_path path yaml file specifies configuration options evaluation. out_path directory write evaluation data . oracle_output Optional data frame oracle output use evaluation. NULL (default), oracle output discovered hub_path via hubData::connect_target_oracle_output(). Supplying pre-loaded data frame remains supported back-compat callers load oracle data .

title: Generate evaluation data for a hub — generate_eval_data
text: (target, eval_set, disaggregation) requested config, scores.csv written out_path/<target_id>/<eval_set_name>[/<>]/ wide format, one row per model per disaggregation key. target configured transform, transformed-scale metrics appear <metric>__<label>-suffixed columns (e.g. wis__log) alongside natural-scale columns. Setting append: false emits suffixed columns. Transform-invariant metrics (interval_coverage_<n> bias, whose values unchanged monotonic transform) always appear natural-scale name regardless append. full configuration schema, see JSON Schema files installed package system.file("schema", package = "hubPredEvalsData").

Transform-invariant metrics (interval_coverage_<n>, bias) unchanged monotonic transform always appear natural-scale name. list dashboard iterates build metric selector. transform: resolved transform target (transform_defaults inheritance applied), list fun, label, append composed human-readable description dashboard can show explain transformed scale user. key absent transform applies, including targets opt transform: false targets whose available output types non-transformable (transform skipped scoring time). config read via read_predevals_config(), config schema transform validation run side effect. caller decides write result.

title: hubPredEvalsData 1.1.1
text: generate_eval_data() longer fails hub’s oracle output includes as_of provenance column (versioned target-data). Oracle output holds single value per observational unit, column dropped scoring rather passed hubEvals::score_model_out(), rejects columns outside task-id keys plus oracle_value (#70).

title: hubPredEvalsData 1.1.0
text: transform_defaults — top-level default transform applied transformable targets. targets[*].transform — per-target transform overrides transform_defaults, literal false opt target . Supported transform functions: log_shift, sqrt, log1p, log, log10, log2. Configured transforms now applied scoring (#40). scores.csv now emitted wide format, transformed-scale metrics <metric>__<label>-suffixed columns (e.g. wis__log). Setting append: false emits suffixed columns. Transform-invariant metrics (interval_coverage_<n> bias) now always reported single, un-suffixed scale scores.csv predevals-options.json, even transform configured. unchanged strictly monotonic transform (interval containment sign forecast-vs-observation error rank-based), transformed-scale column duplicate natural-scale column. Matches invariance set documented hubverse-org/hubDocs#464 (#63). Existing v1.0.1 configs continue validate v1.1.0 without changes. Added generate_predevals_options(), assembles contents predevals-options.json file used initialise predevals dashboard. returns validated config target’s metrics expanded columns present scores.csv (relative-skill metrics, plus transformed-scale <metric>__<label> metrics transform applies) resolved transform block attached (#41, closes #4). generate_eval_data() now discovers oracle output hub_path via hubData::connect_target_oracle_output() oracle_output supplied; argument remains supported back-compat (#51).

title: hubPredEvalsData 1.1.0
text: generate_eval_data() longer fails ordinal pmf targets request rps. ordinal level order now read hub’s tasks.json forwarded hubEvals::score_model_out() scoringutils dispatches data ordinal (#48). read_predevals_config() now warns ordinal-pmf metric (e.g. rps) requested pre-v4 tasks-schema (output_type_id split across required/optional), errors hub’s pmf output_type_id$optional non-empty (#48).

title: hubPredEvalsData 1.0.0

title: hubPredEvalsData 1.0.0
text: Added rounds_idx configuration property specify round entry use hub’s tasks.json file (#26). Hubs multiple rounds now supported.
